modules.label: psexec
modules.tooltip: Runs commands on a remote Windows host based on the PsExec model

fields.label: arguments
fields.description: Any arguments as a single string to use when running the executable.

fields.label: asynchronous
fields.description: Will run the command as a detached process and the module returns immediately after starting the process while the process continues to run in the background. The I(stdout) and I(stderr) return values will be null when this is set to C(yes). The I(stdin) option does not work with this type of process. The I(rc) return value is not set when this is C(yes)

fields.label: connection password
fields.description: The password for I(connection_user). Required if the Kerberos requirements are not installed or the username is a local account to the Windows host. Can be omitted to use a Kerberos principal ticket for the principal set by I(connection_user) if the Kerberos library is installed and the ticket has already been retrieved with the C(kinit) command before.

fields.label: connection timeout
fields.description: The timeout in seconds to wait when receiving the initial SMB negotiate response from the server.

fields.label: connection username
fields.description: The username to use when connecting to the remote Windows host. This user must be a member of the C(Administrators) group of the Windows host. Required if the Kerberos requirements are not installed or the username is a local account to the Windows host. Can be omitted to use the default Kerberos principal ticket in the local credential cache if the Kerberos library is installed. If I(process_username) is not specified, then the remote process will run under a Network Logon under this account.

fields.label: encrypt
fields.description: Will use SMB encryption to encrypt the SMB messages sent to and from the host. This requires the SMB 3 protocol which is only supported from Windows Server 2012 or Windows 8, older versions like Windows 7 or Windows Server 2008 (R2) must set this to C(no) and use no encryption. When setting to C(no), the packets are in plaintext and can be seen by anyone sniffing the network, any process options are included in this.

fields.label: executable
fields.description: The executable to run on the Windows host.

fields.label: hostname
fields.description: The remote Windows host to connect to, can be either an IP address or a hostname.

fields.label: integrity level
fields.description: The integrity level of the process when I(process_username) is defined and is not equal to C(System). When C(default), the default integrity level based on the system setup. When C(elevated), the command will be run with Administrative rights. When C(limited), the command will be forced to run with non-Administrative rights.

fields.label: interactive
fields.description: Will run the process as an interactive process that shows a process Window of the Windows session specified by I(interactive_session). The I(stdout) and I(stderr) return values will be null when this is set to C(yes). The I(stdin) option does not work with this type of process.

fields.label: interactive session
fields.description: The Windows session ID to use when displaying the interactive process on the remote Windows host. This is only valid when I(interactive) is C(yes). The default is C(0) which is the console session of the Windows host.

fields.label: load profile
fields.description: Runs the remote command with the user's profile loaded.

fields.label: priority
fields.description: Set the command's priority on the Windows host. See U(https://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/windows/desktop/ms683211.aspx) for more details.

fields.label: process password
fields.description: The password for I(process_username). Required if I(process_username) is defined and not C(System).

fields.label: process timeout
fields.description: The timeout in seconds that is placed upon the running process. A value of C(0) means no timeout.

fields.label: process username
fields.description: The user to run the process as. This can be set to run the process under an Interactive logon of the specified account which bypasses limitations of a Network logon used when this isn't specified. If omitted then the process is run under the same account as I(connection_username) with a Network logon. Set to C(System) to run as the builtin SYSTEM account, no password is required with this account. If I(encrypt) is C(no), the username and password are sent as a simple XOR scrambled byte string that is not encrypted. No special tools are required to get the username and password just knowledge of the protocol.

fields.label: show ui on logon screen
fields.description: Shows the process UI on the Winlogon secure desktop when I(process_username) is C(System).

fields.label: stdin
fields.description: Data to send on the stdin pipe once the process has started. This option has no effect when I(interactive) or I(asynchronous) is C(yes).

fields.label: working directory
fields.description: Changes the working directory set when starting the process.

modules.label: win_data_deduplication
modules.tooltip: Module to enable Data Deduplication on a volume.

fields.label: drive letter
fields.description: Windows drive letter on which to enable data deduplication.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Wether to enable or disable data deduplication on the selected volume.

modules.label: win_defrag
modules.tooltip: Consolidate fragmented files on local volumes

fields.label: exclude volumes
fields.description: A list of drive letters or mount point paths to exclude from defragmentation.

fields.label: freespace consolidation
fields.description: Perform free space consolidation on the specified volumes.

fields.label: include volumes
fields.description: A list of drive letters or mount point paths of the volumes to be defragmented. If this parameter is omitted, all volumes (not excluded) will be fragmented.

fields.label: parallel
fields.description: Run the operation on each volume in parallel in the background.

fields.label: priority
fields.description: Run the operation at low or normal priority.

modules.label: win_disk_facts
modules.tooltip: Show the attached disks and disk information of the target host

fields.label: filter
fields.description: Allows to filter returned facts by type of disk information. If volumes are selected partitions will be returned as well.

modules.label: win_disk_image
modules.tooltip: Manage ISO/VHD/VHDX mounts on Windows hosts

fields.label: image path
fields.description: Path to an ISO, VHD, or VHDX image on the target Windows host (the file cannot reside on a network share)

fields.label: state
fields.description: Whether the image should be present as a drive-letter mount or not.

modules.label: win_eventlog_entry
modules.tooltip: Write entries to Windows event logs

fields.label: category
fields.description: A numeric task category associated with the category message file for the log source.

fields.label: entry type
fields.description: Indicates the entry being written to the log is of a specific type.

fields.label: event id
fields.description: The numeric event identifier for the entry. Value must be between 0 and 65535.

fields.label: log
fields.description: Name of the event log to write an entry to.

fields.label: message
fields.description: The message for the given log entry.

fields.label: raw data
fields.description: Binary data associated with the log entry. Value must be a comma-separated array of 8-bit unsigned integers (0 to 255).

fields.label: source
fields.description: Name of the log source to indicate where the entry is from.

modules.label: win_file_version
modules.tooltip: Get DLL or EXE file build version

fields.label: path
fields.description: File to get version. Always provide absolute path.

modules.label: win_firewall_rule
modules.tooltip: Windows firewall automation

fields.label: action
fields.description: What to do with the items this rule is for. Defaults to C(allow) when creating a new rule.

fields.label: description
fields.description: Description for the firewall rule.

fields.label: direction
fields.description: Whether this rule is for inbound or outbound traffic. Defaults to C(in) when creating a new rule.

fields.label: enabled
fields.description: Whether this firewall rule is enabled or disabled. Defaults to C(true) when creating a new rule.

fields.label: group
fields.description: The group name for the rule. If I(name) is not specified then the module will set the firewall options for all the rules in this group.

fields.label: icmp type code
fields.description: The ICMP types and codes for the rule. This is only valid when I(protocol) is C(icmpv4) or C(icmpv6). Each entry follows the format C(type:code) where C(type) is the type number and C(code) is the code number for that type or C(*) for all codes. Set the value to just C(*) to apply the rule for all ICMP type codes. See U(https://www.iana.org/assignments/icmp-parameters/icmp-parameters.xhtml) for a list of ICMP types and the codes that apply to them. ICMP types and codes for IPV6 can be found at U(https://www.iana.org/assignments/icmpv6-parameters/icmpv6-parameters.xhtml).

fields.label: localip
fields.description: The local ip address this rule applies to. Set to C(any) to apply to all local ip addresses. Defaults to C(any) when creating a new rule.

fields.label: localport
fields.description: The local port this rule applies to. Set to C(any) to apply to all local ports. Defaults to C(any) when creating a new rule. Must have I(protocol) set

fields.label: name
fields.description: The rule's display name. This is required unless I(group) is specified.

fields.label: profiles
fields.description: The profile this rule applies to. Defaults to C(domain,private,public) when creating a new rule.

fields.label: program
fields.description: The program this rule applies to. Set to C(any) to apply to all programs. Defaults to C(any) when creating a new rule.

fields.label: protocol
fields.description: The protocol this rule applies to. Set to C(any) to apply to all services. Defaults to C(any) when creating a new rule.

fields.label: remoteip
fields.description: The remote ip address/range this rule applies to. Set to C(any) to apply to all remote ip addresses. Defaults to C(any) when creating a new rule.

fields.label: remoteport
fields.description: The remote port this rule applies to. Set to C(any) to apply to all remote ports. Defaults to C(any) when creating a new rule. Must have I(protocol) set

fields.label: service
fields.description: The service this rule applies to. Set to C(any) to apply to all services. Defaults to C(any) when creating a new rule.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Should this rule be added or removed.

modules.label: win_format
modules.tooltip: Formats an existing volume or a new volume on an existing partition on Windows

fields.label: allocation unit size
fields.description: Specifies the cluster size to use when formatting the volume. If no cluster size is specified when you format a partition, defaults are selected based on the size of the partition. This value must be a multiple of the physical sector size of the disk.

fields.label: compress
fields.description: Enable compression on the resulting NTFS volume. NTFS compression is not supported where I(allocation_unit_size) is more than 4096.

fields.label: drive letter
fields.description: Used to specify the drive letter of the volume to be formatted.

fields.label: file system
fields.description: Used to specify the file system to be used when formatting the target volume.

fields.label: force
fields.description: Specify if formatting should be forced for volumes that are not created from new partitions or if the source and target file system are different.

fields.label: full
fields.description: A full format writes to every sector of the disk, takes much longer to perform than the default (quick) format, and is not recommended on storage that is thinly provisioned. Specify C(true) for full format.

fields.label: integrity streams
fields.description: Enable integrity streams on the resulting ReFS volume.

fields.label: label
fields.description: Used to specify the label of the volume to be formatted.

fields.label: large frs
fields.description: Specifies that large File Record System (FRS) should be used.

fields.label: new label
fields.description: Used to specify the new file system label of the formatted volume.

fields.label: path
fields.description: Used to specify the path to the volume to be formatted.

modules.label: win_iis_virtualdirectory
modules.tooltip: Configures a virtual directory in IIS

fields.label: application
fields.description: The application under which the virtual directory is created or exists.

fields.label: connect as
fields.description: The type of authentication to use for the virtual directory. Either C(pass_through) or C(specific_user) If C(pass_through), IIS will use the identity of the user or application pool identity to access the physical path. If C(specific_user), IIS will use the credentials provided in I(username) and I(password) to access the physical path.

fields.label: name
fields.description: The name of the virtual directory to create or remove.

fields.label: physical path
fields.description: The physical path to the folder in which the new virtual directory is created. The specified folder must already exist.

fields.label: site
fields.description: The site name under which the virtual directory is created or exists.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Whether to add or remove the specified virtual directory. Removing will remove the virtual directory and all under it (Recursively).

modules.label: win_iis_webapplication
modules.tooltip: Configures IIS web applications

fields.label: application pool
fields.description: The application pool in which the new site executes. If not specified, the application pool of the current website will be used.

fields.label: connect as
fields.description: The type of authentication to use for this application. Either C(pass_through) or C(specific_user) If C(pass_through), IIS will use the identity of the user or application pool identity to access the file system or network. If C(specific_user), IIS will use the credentials provided in I(username) and I(password) to access the file system or network.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Name of the web application.

fields.label: physical path
fields.description: The physical path on the remote host to use for the new application. The specified folder must already exist.

fields.label: site
fields.description: Name of the site on which the application is created.

fields.label: state
fields.description: State of the web application.

modules.label: win_iis_webapppool
modules.tooltip: Configure IIS Web Application Pools

fields.label: attributes
fields.description: This field is a free form dictionary value for the application pool attributes. These attributes are based on the naming standard at U(https://www.iis.net/configreference/system.applicationhost/applicationpools/add#005), see the examples section for more details on how to set this. You can also set the attributes of child elements like cpu and processModel, see the examples to see how it is done. While you can use the numeric values for enums it is recommended to use the enum name itself, e.g. use SpecificUser instead of 3 for processModel.identityType. managedPipelineMode may be either "Integrated" or "Classic". startMode may be either "OnDemand" or "AlwaysRunning". Use C(state) module parameter to modify the state of the app pool. When trying to set 'processModel.password' and you receive a 'Value does fall within the expected range' error, you have a corrupted keystore. Please follow U(http://structuredsight.com/2014/10/26/im-out-of-range-youre-out-of-range/) to help fix your host.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Name of the application pool.

fields.label: state
fields.description: The state of the application pool. If C(absent) will ensure the app pool is removed. If C(present) will ensure the app pool is configured and exists. If C(restarted) will ensure the app pool exists and will restart, this is never idempotent. If C(started) will ensure the app pool exists and is started. If C(stopped) will ensure the app pool exists and is stopped.

modules.label: win_iis_webbinding
modules.tooltip: Configures a IIS Web site binding

fields.label: certificate hash
fields.description: Certificate hash (thumbprint) for the SSL binding. The certificate hash is the unique identifier for the certificate.

fields.label: certificate store name
fields.description: Name of the certificate store where the certificate for the binding is located.

fields.label: host header
fields.description: The host header to bind to / use for the new site. If you are creating/removing a catch-all binding, omit this parameter rather than defining it as '*'.

fields.label: ip
fields.description: The IP address to bind to / use for the new site.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Names of web site.

fields.label: protocol
fields.description: The protocol to be used for the Web binding (usually HTTP, HTTPS, or FTP).

fields.label: ssl flags
fields.description: This parameter is only valid on Server 2012 and newer. Primarily used for enabling and disabling server name indication (SNI). Set to C(0) to disable SNI. Set to C(1) to enable SNI.

fields.label: state
fields.description: State of the binding.

modules.label: win_iis_website
modules.tooltip: Configures a IIS Web site

fields.label: application pool
fields.description: The application pool in which the new site executes.

fields.label: hostname
fields.description: The host header to bind to / use for the new site.

fields.label: ip
fields.description: The IP address to bind to / use for the new site.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Names of web site.

fields.label: parameters
fields.description: Custom site Parameters from string where properties are separated by a pipe and property name/values by colon Ex. "foo:1|bar:2" Some custom parameters that you can use are listed below, this isn't a definitive list but some common parameters. C(logfile.directory) - Physical path to store Logs, e.g. C(D:\IIS-LOGs\). C(logfile.period) - Log file rollover scheduled accepting these values, how frequently the log file should be rolled-over, e.g. C(Hourly, Daily, Weekly, Monthly). C(logfile.LogFormat) - Log file format, by default IIS uses C(W3C). C(logfile.truncateSize) - The size at which the log file contents will be trunsted, expressed in bytes.

fields.label: physical path
fields.description: The physical path on the remote host to use for the new site. The specified folder must already exist.

fields.label: site id
fields.description: Explicitly set the IIS numeric ID for a site. Note that this value cannot be changed after the website has been created.

fields.label: state
fields.description: State of the web site

modules.label: win_initialize_disk
modules.tooltip: Initializes disks on Windows Server

fields.label: disk number
fields.description: Used to specify the disk number of the disk to be initialized.

fields.label: force
fields.description: Specify if initializing should be forced for disks that are already initialized.

fields.label: online
fields.description: If the disk is offline and/or readonly update the disk to be online and not readonly.

fields.label: path
fields.description: Used to specify the path to the disk to be initialized.

fields.label: style
fields.description: The partition style to use for the disk. Valid options are mbr or gpt.

fields.label: uniqueid
fields.description: Used to specify the uniqueid of the disk to be initialized.

modules.label: win_lineinfile
modules.tooltip: Ensure a particular line is in a file, or replace an existing line using a back-referenced regular expression

fields.label: backrefs
fields.description: Used with C(state=present). If set, line can contain backreferences (both positional and named) that will get populated if the C(regexp) matches. This flag changes the operation of the module slightly; C(insertbefore) and C(insertafter) will be ignored, and if the C(regexp) doesn't match anywhere in the file, the file will be left unchanged. If the C(regexp) does match, the last matching line will be replaced by the expanded line parameter.

fields.label: backup
fields.description: Determine whether a backup should be created. When set to C(yes), create a backup file including the timestamp information so you can get the original file back if you somehow clobbered it incorrectly.

fields.label: create
fields.description: Used with C(state=present). If specified, the file will be created if it does not already exist. By default it will fail if the file is missing.

fields.label: encoding
fields.description: Specifies the encoding of the source text file to operate on (and thus what the output encoding will be). The default of C(auto) will cause the module to auto-detect the encoding of the source file and ensure that the modified file is written with the same encoding. An explicit encoding can be passed as a string that is a valid value to pass to the .NET framework System.Text.Encoding.GetEncoding() method - see U(https://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/system.text.encoding%28v=vs.110%29.aspx). This is mostly useful with C(create=yes) if you want to create a new file with a specific encoding. If C(create=yes) is specified without a specific encoding, the default encoding (UTF-8, no BOM) will be used.

fields.label: insertafter
fields.description: Used with C(state=present). If specified, the line will be inserted after the last match of specified regular expression. A special value is available; C(EOF) for inserting the line at the end of the file. If specified regular expression has no matches, EOF will be used instead. May not be used with C(backrefs).

fields.label: insertbefore
fields.description: Used with C(state=present). If specified, the line will be inserted before the last match of specified regular expression. A value is available; C(BOF) for inserting the line at the beginning of the file. If specified regular expression has no matches, the line will be inserted at the end of the file. May not be used with C(backrefs).

fields.label: line
fields.description: Required for C(state=present). The line to insert/replace into the file. If C(backrefs) is set, may contain backreferences that will get expanded with the C(regexp) capture groups if the regexp matches. Be aware that the line is processed first on the controller and thus is dependent on yaml quoting rules. Any double quoted line will have control characters, such as '\r\n', expanded. To print such characters literally, use single or no quotes.

fields.label: newline
fields.description: Specifies the line separator style to use for the modified file. This defaults to the windows line separator (C(\r\n)). Note that the indicated line separator will be used for file output regardless of the original line separator that appears in the input file.

fields.label: path
fields.description: The path of the file to modify. Note that the Windows path delimiter C(\) must be escaped as C(\\) when the line is double quoted.

fields.label: regex
fields.description: The regular expression to look for in every line of the file. For C(state=present), the pattern to replace if found; only the last line found will be replaced. For C(state=absent), the pattern of the line to remove. Uses .NET compatible regular expressions; see U(https://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/hs600312%28v=vs.110%29.aspx).

fields.label: state
fields.description: Whether the line should be there or not.

fields.label: validate
fields.description: Validation to run before copying into place. Use %s in the command to indicate the current file to validate. The command is passed securely so shell features like expansion and pipes won't work.

modules.label: win_msg
modules.tooltip: Sends a message to logged in users on Windows hosts

fields.label: display seconds
fields.description: How long to wait for receiver to acknowledge message, in seconds.

fields.label: msg
fields.description: The text of the message to be displayed. The message must be less than 256 characters.

fields.label: to
fields.description: Who to send the message to. Can be a username, sessionname or sessionid.

fields.label: wait
fields.description: Whether to wait for users to respond.  Module will only wait for the number of seconds specified in display_seconds or 10 seconds if not specified. However, if I(wait) is C(yes), the message is sent to each logged on user in turn, waiting for the user to either press 'ok' or for the timeout to elapse before moving on to the next user.

modules.label: win_net_adapter_feature
modules.tooltip: Enable or disable certain network adapters.

fields.label: component id
fields.description: Specify the below component_id of network adapters. component_id (DisplayName) C(ms_implat) (Microsoft Network Adapter Multiplexor Protocol) C(ms_lltdio) (Link-Layer Topology Discovery Mapper I/O Driver) C(ms_tcpip6) (Internet Protocol Version 6 (TCP/IPv6)) C(ms_tcpip) (Internet Protocol Version 4 (TCP/IPv4)) C(ms_lldp) (Microsoft LLDP Protocol Driver) C(ms_rspndr) (Link-Layer Topology Discovery Responder) C(ms_msclient) (Client for Microsoft Networks) C(ms_pacer) (QoS Packet Scheduler) If you'd like to set custom adapters like 'Juniper Network Service', get the I(component_id) by running the C(Get-NetAdapterBinding) cmdlet.

fields.label: interface
fields.description: Name of Network Adapter Interface. For example, C(Ethernet0) or C(*).

fields.label: state
fields.description: Specify the state of ms_tcpip6 of interfaces.

modules.label: win_netbios
modules.tooltip: Manage NetBIOS over TCP/IP settings on Windows.

fields.label: adapter names
fields.description: List of adapter names for which to manage NetBIOS settings. If this option is omitted then configuration is applied to all adapters on the system. The adapter name used is the connection caption in the Network Control Panel or via C(Get-NetAdapter), eg C(Ethernet 2).

fields.label: state
fields.description: Whether NetBIOS should be enabled, disabled, or default (use setting from DHCP server or if static IP address is assigned enable NetBIOS).

modules.label: win_nssm
modules.tooltip: Install a service using NSSM

fields.label: app environment
fields.description: Key/Value pairs which will be added to the environment of the service application.

fields.label: app rotate bytes
fields.description: NSSM will not rotate any file which is smaller than the configured number of bytes.

fields.label: app rotate online
fields.description: If set to 1, nssm can rotate files which grow to the configured file size limit while the service is running.

fields.label: app stop method console
fields.description: Time to wait after sending Control-C.

fields.label: app stop method skip
fields.description: To disable service shutdown methods, set to the sum of one or more of the numbers 1 - Don't send Control-C to the console. 2 - Don't send WM_CLOSE to windows. 4 - Don't send WM_QUIT to threads. 8 - Don't call TerminateProcess().

fields.label: application
fields.description: The application binary to run as a service Required when I(state) is C(present), C(started), C(stopped), or C(restarted).

fields.label: arguments
fields.description: Parameters to be passed to the application when it starts. This can be either a simple string or a list.

fields.label: dependencies
fields.description: Service dependencies that has to be started to trigger startup, separated by comma.

fields.label: description
fields.description: The description to set for the service.

fields.label: display name
fields.description: The display name to set for the service.

fields.label: executable
fields.description: The location of the NSSM utility (in case it is not located in your PATH).

fields.label: name
fields.description: Name of the service to operate on.

fields.label: start mode
fields.description: If C(auto) is selected, the service will start at bootup. C(delayed) causes a delayed but automatic start after boot. C(manual) means that the service will start only when another service needs it. C(disabled) means that the service will stay off, regardless if it is needed or not.

fields.label: state
fields.description: State of the service on the system.

fields.label: stderr file
fields.description: Path to receive error output.

fields.label: stdout file
fields.description: Path to receive output.

fields.label: working directory
fields.description: The working directory to run the service executable from (defaults to the directory containing the application binary)

modules.label: win_pagefile
modules.tooltip: Query or change pagefile configuration

fields.label: automatic
fields.description: Configures AutomaticManagedPagefile for the entire system.

fields.label: drive
fields.description: The drive of the pagefile.

fields.label: initial size
fields.description: The initial size of the pagefile in megabytes.

fields.label: maximum size
fields.description: The maximum size of the pagefile in megabytes.

fields.label: override
fields.description: Override the current pagefile on the drive.

fields.label: remove all
fields.description: Remove all pagefiles in the system, not including automatic managed.

fields.label: state
fields.description: State of the pagefile.

fields.label: system managed
fields.description: Configures current pagefile to be managed by the system.

fields.label: test path
fields.description: Use Test-Path on the drive to make sure the drive is accessible before creating the pagefile.

modules.label: win_partition
modules.tooltip: Creates, changes and removes partitions on Windows Server

fields.label: active
fields.description: Specifies if the partition is active and can be used to start the system. This property is only valid when the disk's partition style is MBR.

fields.label: disk number
fields.description: Disk number is mandatory for creating new partitions. A combination of I(disk_number) and I(partition_number) can be used to specify the partition instead of I(drive_letter) if required.

fields.label: drive letter
fields.description: Used for accessing partitions if I(disk_number) and I(partition_number) are not provided. Use C(auto) for automatically assigning a drive letter, or a letter A-Z for manually assigning a drive letter to a new partition. If not specified, no drive letter is assigned when creating a new partition.

fields.label: gpt type
fields.description: Specify the partition's GPT type if the disk's partition style is GPT. This only applies to new partitions. This does not relate to the partitions file system formatting.

fields.label: hidden
fields.description: Hides the target partition, making it undetectable by the mount manager.

fields.label: mbr type
fields.description: Specify the partition's MBR type if the disk's partition style is MBR. This only applies to new partitions. This does not relate to the partitions file system formatting.

fields.label: offline
fields.description: Sets the partition offline. Adding a mount point (such as a drive letter) will cause the partition to go online again.

fields.label: partition number
fields.description: Used in conjunction with I(disk_number) to uniquely identify a partition.

fields.label: partition size
fields.description: Specify size of the partition in B, KB, KiB, MB, MiB, GB, GiB, TB or TiB. Use -1 to specify maximum supported size. Partition size is mandatory for creating a new partition but not for updating or deleting a partition. The decimal SI prefixes kilo, mega, giga, tera, etc., are powers of 10^3 = 1000. The binary prefixes kibi, mebi, gibi, tebi, etc. respectively refer to the corresponding power of 2^10 = 1024. Thus, a gigabyte (GB) is 1000000000 (1000^3) bytes while 1 gibibyte (GiB) is 1073741824 (1024^3) bytes.

fields.label: read only
fields.description: Make the partition read only, restricting changes from being made to the partition.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Used to specify the state of the partition. Use C(absent) to specify if a partition should be removed and C(present) to specify if the partition should be created or updated.

modules.label: win_pester
modules.tooltip: Run Pester tests on Windows hosts

fields.label: output file
fields.description: Generates an output test report.

fields.label: output format
fields.description: Format of the test report to be generated. This parameter is to be used with output_file option.

fields.label: path
fields.description: Path to a pester test file or a folder where tests can be found. If the path is a folder, the module will consider all ps1 files as Pester tests.

fields.label: tags
fields.description: Runs only tests in Describe blocks with specified Tags values. Accepts multiple comma separated tags.

fields.label: test parameters
fields.description: Allows to specify parameters to the test script.

fields.label: version
fields.description: Minimum version of the pester module that has to be available on the remote host.

modules.label: win_power_plan
modules.tooltip: Changes the power plan of a Windows system

fields.label: guid
fields.description: String value that indicates the desired power plan by guid. The power plan must already be present on the system. For out of box guids see U(https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/win32/power/power-policy-settings).

fields.label: name
fields.description: String value that indicates the desired power plan by name. The power plan must already be present on the system. Commonly there will be options for C(balanced) and C(high performance).

modules.label: win_psexec
modules.tooltip: Runs commands (remotely) as another (privileged) user

fields.label: chdir
fields.description: Run the command from this (remote) directory.

fields.label: command
fields.description: The command line to run through PsExec (limited to 260 characters).

fields.label: elevated
fields.description: Run the command with elevated privileges.

fields.label: executable
fields.description: The location of the PsExec utility (in case it is not located in your PATH).

fields.label: hostnames
fields.description: The hostnames to run the command. If not provided, the command is run locally.

fields.label: interactive
fields.description: Run the program so that it interacts with the desktop on the remote system.

fields.label: limited
fields.description: Run the command as limited user (strips the Administrators group and allows only privileges assigned to the Users group).

fields.label: nobanner
fields.description: Do not display the startup banner and copyright message. This only works for specific versions of the PsExec binary.

fields.label: noprofile
fields.description: Run the command without loading the account's profile.

fields.label: priority
fields.description: Used to run the command at a different priority.

fields.label: session
fields.description: Specifies the session ID to use. This parameter works in conjunction with I(interactive). It has no effect when I(interactive) is set to C(no).

fields.label: system
fields.description: Run the remote command in the System account.

fields.label: wait
fields.description: Wait for the application to terminate. Only use for non-interactive applications.

modules.label: win_psmodule
modules.tooltip: Adds or removes a Windows PowerShell module

fields.label: accept license
fields.description: Accepts the module's license. Required for modules that require license acceptance, since interactively answering the prompt is not possible. Corresponds to the C(-AcceptLicense) parameter of C(Install-Module). Installation of a module or a dependency that requires license acceptance cannot be detected in check mode, but will cause a failure at runtime unless I(accept_license=true).

fields.label: allow clobber
fields.description: If C(yes) allows install modules that contains commands those have the same names as commands that already exists.

fields.label: allow prerelease
fields.description: If C(yes) installs modules marked as prereleases. It doesn't work with the parameters C(minimum_version) and/or C(maximum_version). It doesn't work with the C(state) set to absent.

fields.label: force
fields.description: Overrides warning messages about module installation conflicts If there is an existing module with the same name and version, force overwrites that version Corresponds to the C(-Force) parameter of C(Install-Module). If module as dependency, it will also force reinstallation of dependency. Updating them to latest if version isn't specified in module manifest.

fields.label: maximum version
fields.description: The maximum version of the PowerShell module that has to be installed.

fields.label: minimum version
fields.description: The minimum version of the PowerShell module that has to be installed.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Name of the Windows PowerShell module that has to be installed.

fields.label: repository
fields.description: Name of the custom repository to use.

fields.label: required version
fields.description: The exact version of the PowerShell module that has to be installed.

fields.label: skip publisher check
fields.description: If C(yes), allows you to install a different version of a module that already exists on your computer in the case when a different one is not digitally signed by a trusted publisher and the newest existing module is digitally signed by a trusted publisher.

fields.label: state
fields.description: If C(present) a new module is installed. If C(absent) a module is removed. If C(latest) a module is updated to the newest version.

modules.label: win_psmodule_info
modules.tooltip: Gather information about PowerShell Modules

fields.label: name
fields.description: The name of the module to retrieve. Supports any wildcard pattern supported by C(Get-Module). If omitted then all modules will returned.

fields.label: repository
fields.description: The name of the PSRepository the modules were installed from. This acts as a filter against the modules that would be returned based on the I(name) option. Modules that were not installed from a repository will not be returned if this option is set. Only modules installed from a registered repository will be returned. If the repository was re-registered after module installation with a new C(SourceLocation), this will not match.

modules.label: win_psrepository
modules.tooltip: Adds, removes or updates a Windows PowerShell repository.

fields.label: force
fields.description: If C(True), any differences from the desired state will result in the repository being unregistered, and then re-registered. I(force) has no effect when I(state=absent). See notes for additional context.

fields.label: installation policy
fields.description: Sets the C(InstallationPolicy) of a repository. Will default to C(trusted) when creating a new repository or used with I(force=True).

fields.label: name
fields.description: Name of the repository to work with.

fields.label: proxy
fields.description: Proxy to use for repository.

fields.label: publish location
fields.description: Specifies the URI for publishing modules to this repository.

fields.label: script publish location
fields.description: Specifies the URI for publishing scripts to this repository.

fields.label: script source location
fields.description: Specifies the URI for discovering and installing scripts from this repository.

fields.label: source location
fields.description: Specifies the URI for discovering and installing modules from this repository. A URI can be a NuGet server feed (most common situation), HTTP, HTTPS, FTP or file location. Required when registering a new repository or using I(force=True).

fields.label: state
fields.description: If C(present) a new repository is added or updated. If C(absent) a repository is removed.

modules.label: win_psrepository_copy
modules.tooltip: Copies registered PSRepositories to other user profiles

fields.label: exclude
fields.description: The names of repositories to exclude. Names are interpreted as wildcards. If a name matches both an include (I(name)) and I(exclude), it will be excluded.

fields.label: exclude profiles
fields.description: The names of user profiles to exclude. If a profile matches both an include (I(profiles)) and I(exclude_profiles), it will be excluded. By default, the service account profiles are excluded. To explcitly exclude nothing, set I(exclude_profiles=[]).

fields.label: force
fields.description: If C(True), this deletes repositories that are not present in the source.

fields.label: name
fields.description: The names of repositories to copy. Names are interpreted as wildcards.

fields.label: profiles
fields.description: The names of user profiles to populate with repositories. Names are interpreted as wildcards. The C(Default) profile can also be matched. The C(Public) and C(All Users) profiles cannot be targeted, as PSRepositories are not loaded from them.

fields.label: source
fields.description: The full path to the source repositories XML file. Defaults to the repositories registered to the current user.

modules.label: win_psrepository_info
modules.tooltip: Gather information about PSRepositories

fields.label: name
fields.description: The name of the repository to retrieve. Supports any wildcard pattern supported by C(Get-PSRepository). If omitted then all repositories will returned.

modules.label: win_psscript
modules.tooltip: Install and manage PowerShell scripts from a PSRepository

fields.label: allow prerelease
fields.description: If C(yes) installs scripts flagged as prereleases.

fields.label: maximum version
fields.description: The maximum version of the script to install. Cannot be used when I(state=latest).

fields.label: minimum version
fields.description: The minimum version of the script to install. Cannot be used when I(state=latest).

fields.label: name
fields.description: The name of the script you want to install or remove.

fields.label: repository
fields.description: The registered name of the repository you want to install from. Cannot be used when I(state=absent). If ommitted, all repositories will be searched. To register a repository, use M(community.windows.win_psrepository).

fields.label: required version
fields.description: The exact version of the script to install. Cannot be used with I(minimum_version) or I(maximum_version). Cannot be used when I(state=latest).

fields.label: scope
fields.description: Determines whether the script is installed for only the C(current_user) or for C(all_users).

fields.label: source password
fields.description: The password portion of the credential required to access the repository. Must be used together with I(source_username).

fields.label: source username
fields.description: The username portion of the credential required to access the repository. Must be used together with I(source_password).

fields.label: state
fields.description: The desired state of the script. C(absent) removes the script. C(latest) will ensure the most recent version available is installed. C(present) only installs if the script is missing.

modules.label: win_psscript_info
modules.tooltip: Gather information about installed PowerShell Scripts

fields.label: name
fields.description: The name of the script. Supports any wildcard pattern supported by C(Get-InstalledScript). If omitted then all scripts will returned.

fields.label: repository
fields.description: The name of the PSRepository the scripts were installed from. This acts as a filter against the scripts that would be returned based on the I(name) option. Only scripts installed from a registered repository will be returned. If the repository was re-registered after script installation with a new C(SourceLocation), this will not match.

modules.label: win_pssession_configuration
modules.tooltip: Manage PSSession Configurations

fields.label: access mode
fields.description: Controls whether the session configuration allows connection from the C(local) machine only, both local and C(remote), or none (C(disabled)).

fields.label: alias definitions
fields.description: A dict that defines aliases for each session.

fields.label: assemblies to load
fields.description: The assemblies that should be loaded into each session.

fields.label: async poll
fields.description: Sets a delay in seconds between each check of the asynchronous execution status. Replicates the functionality of the C(poll) keyword. Has no effect in check mode. I(async_poll=0) is not supported.

fields.label: async timeout
fields.description: Sets a timeout for how long in seconds to wait for asynchronous module execution and waiting for the connection to recover. Replicates the functionality of the C(async) keyword. Has no effect in check mode.

fields.label: author
fields.description: The author of the session configuration. This value is metadata and does not affect the functionality of the session configuration. If not set, a value may be generated automatically. See also I(lenient_config_fields).

fields.label: company name
fields.description: The company that authored the session configuration. This value is metadata and does not affect the functionality of the session configuration. If not set, a value may be generated automatically. See also I(lenient_config_fields).

fields.label: copyright
fields.description: The copyright statement of the session configuration. This value is metadata and does not affect the functionality of the session configuration. If not set, a value may be generated automatically. See also I(lenient_config_fields).

fields.label: description
fields.description: The description of the session configuration. This value is metadata and does not affect the functionality of the session configuration. See also I(lenient_config_fields).

fields.label: environment variables
fields.description: A dict that defines environment variables for each session.

fields.label: execution policy
fields.description: The execution policy controlling script execution in the PowerShell session.

fields.label: formats to process
fields.description: Paths to format definition files to process for each session.

fields.label: function definitions
fields.description: A dict that defines functions for each session.

fields.label: group managed service account
fields.description: If the session will run as a group managed service account (gMSA) then this is the name. Do not use I(run_as_credential_username) and I(run_as_credential_password) to specify a gMSA.

fields.label: guid
fields.description: The GUID (UUID) of the session configuration file. This value is metadata, so it only matters if you use it externally. If not set, a value will be generated automatically. Acceptable GUID formats are flexible. Any string of 32 hexadecimal digits will be accepted, with all hyphens C(-) and opening/closing C({}) ignored. See also I(lenient_config_fields).

fields.label: language mode
fields.description: Determines the language mode of the PowerShell session.

fields.label: lenient config fields
fields.description: Some fields used in the session configuration do not affect its function, and are sometimes auto-generated when not specified. To avoid unnecessarily changing the configuration on each run, the values of these options will only be enforced when they are explicitly specified.

fields.label: maximum received data size per command mb
fields.description: Sets the maximum received data size per command in MB. Must fit into a double precision floating point value.

fields.label: maximum received object size mb
fields.description: Sets the maximum object size in MB. Must fit into a double precision floating point value.

fields.label: modules to import
fields.description: A list of modules that should be imported into the session. Any valid PowerShell module spec can be used here, so simple str names or dicts can be used. If a dict is used, no snake_case conversion is done, so the original PowerShell names must be used.

fields.label: mount user drive
fields.description: If C(yes) the session creates and mounts a user-specific PSDrive for use with file transfers.

fields.label: name
fields.description: The name of the session configuration to manage.

fields.label: powershell version
fields.description: The minimum required PowerShell version for this session. Must be a valid .Net System.Version string. This only works if targeting PowerShell 5.1, PowerShell 7.x does not support this option.

fields.label: processor architecure
fields.description: The processor architecture of the session (32 bit vs. 64 bit).

fields.label: required groups
fields.description: For JEA sessions, defines conditional access rules about which groups a connecting user must belong to. For more information see U(https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/scripting/learn/remoting/jea/session-configurations#conditional-access-rules).

fields.label: role definitions
fields.description: A dict defining the roles for JEA sessions. For more information see U(https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/scripting/learn/remoting/jea/session-configurations#role-definitions).

fields.label: run as credential password
fields.description: The password for I(run_as_credential_username).

fields.label: run as credential username
fields.description: Used to set a RunAs account for the session. All commands executed in the session will be run as this user. To use a gMSA, see I(group_managed_service_account). To use a virtual account, see I(run_as_virtual_account) and I(run_as_virtual_account_groups). Status will always be C(changed) when a RunAs credential is set because the password cannot be retrieved for comparison.

fields.label: run as virtual account
fields.description: If C(yes) the session runs as a virtual account. Do not use I(run_as_credential_username) and I(run_as_credential_password) to specify a virtual account.

fields.label: run as virtual account groups
fields.description: If I(run_as_virtual_account=yes) this is a list of groups to add the virtual account to.

fields.label: schema version
fields.description: The schema version of the session configuration file. If not set, a value will be generated automatically. Must be a valid .Net System.Version string.

fields.label: scripts to process
fields.description: A list of paths to script files ending in C(.ps1) that should be applied to the session.

fields.label: security descriptor sddl
fields.description: An SDDL string that controls which users and groups can connect to the session. If I(role_definitions) is specified the security descriptor will be set based on that. If this option is not specified the default security descriptor will be applied.

fields.label: session type
fields.description: Controls what type of session this is.

fields.label: startup script
fields.description: A script that gets run on session startup.

fields.label: state
fields.description: The desired state of the configuration.

fields.label: thread apartment state
fields.description: The apartment state for the PowerShell session.

fields.label: thread options
fields.description: Sets thread options for the session.

fields.label: transcript directory
fields.description: Automatic session transcripts will be written to this directory.

fields.label: types to process
fields.description: Paths to type definition files to process for each session.

fields.label: use shared process
fields.description: If C(yes) then the session shares a process for each session.

fields.label: user drive maximum size
fields.description: The maximum size of the user drive in bytes. Must fit into an Int64.

fields.label: variable definitions
fields.description: A list of dicts where each elements defines a variable for each session.

fields.label: visible aliases
fields.description: The aliases that can be used in the session. For more information see U(https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/scripting/learn/remoting/jea/role-capabilities).

fields.label: visible cmdlets
fields.description: The cmdlets that can be used in the session. The elements can be simple names or complex command specifications. For more information see U(https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/scripting/learn/remoting/jea/role-capabilities).

fields.label: visible external commands
fields.description: The external commands and scripts that can be used in the session. For more information see U(https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/scripting/learn/remoting/jea/role-capabilities).

fields.label: visible functions
fields.description: The functions that can be used in the session. The elements can be simple names or complex command specifications. For more information see U(https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/scripting/learn/remoting/jea/role-capabilities).

modules.label: win_rabbitmq_plugin
modules.tooltip: Manage RabbitMQ plugins

fields.label: names
fields.description: Comma-separated list of plugin names.

fields.label: new only
fields.description: Only enable missing plugins. Does not disable plugins that are not in the names list.

fields.label: prefix
fields.description: Specify a custom install prefix to a Rabbit.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Specify if plugins are to be enabled or disabled.

modules.label: win_rds_cap
modules.tooltip: Manage Connection Authorization Policies (CAP) on a Remote Desktop Gateway server

fields.label: allow only sdrts servers
fields.description: Specifies whether connections are allowed only to Remote Desktop Session Host servers that enforce Remote Desktop Gateway redirection policy.

fields.label: auth method
fields.description: Specifies how the RD Gateway server authenticates users. When a new CAP is created, the default value is C(password).

fields.label: computer groups
fields.description: A list of computer groups that is allowed to connect to the Remote Gateway server.

fields.label: idle timeout
fields.description: Specifies the time interval, in minutes, after which an idle session is disconnected. A value of zero disables idle timeout.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Name of the connection authorization policy.

fields.label: order
fields.description: Evaluation order of the policy. The CAP in which I(order) is set to a value of '1' is evaluated first. By default, a newly created CAP will take the first position. If the given value exceed the total number of existing policies, the policy will take the last position but the evaluation order will be capped to this number.

fields.label: redirect clipboard
fields.description: Allow clipboard redirection.

fields.label: redirect drives
fields.description: Allow disk drive redirection.

fields.label: redirect pnp
fields.description: Allow Plug and Play devices redirection.

fields.label: redirect printers
fields.description: Allow printers redirection.

fields.label: redirect serial
fields.description: Allow serial port redirection.

fields.label: session timeout
fields.description: The maximum time, in minutes, that a session can be idle. A value of zero disables session timeout.

fields.label: session timeout action
fields.description: The action the server takes when a session times out. C(disconnect): disconnect the session. C(reauth): silently reauthenticate and reauthorize the session.

fields.label: state
fields.description: The state of connection authorization policy. If C(absent) will ensure the policy is removed. If C(present) will ensure the policy is configured and exists. If C(enabled) will ensure the policy is configured, exists and enabled. If C(disabled) will ensure the policy is configured, exists, but disabled.

fields.label: user groups
fields.description: A list of user groups that is allowed to connect to the Remote Gateway server. Required when a new CAP is created.

modules.label: win_rds_rap
modules.tooltip: Manage Resource Authorization Policies (RAP) on a Remote Desktop Gateway server

fields.label: allowed ports
fields.description: List of port numbers through which connections are allowed for this policy. To allow connections through any port, specify 'any'.

fields.label: computer group
fields.description: The computer group name that is associated with this resource authorization policy (RAP). This is required when I(computer_group_type) is C(rdg_group) or C(ad_network_resource_group).

fields.label: computer group type
fields.description: The computer group type: C(rdg_group): RD Gateway-managed group C(ad_network_resource_group): Active Directory Domain Services network resource group C(allow_any): Allow users to connect to any network resource.

fields.label: description
fields.description: Optional description of the resource authorization policy.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Name of the resource authorization policy.

fields.label: state
fields.description: The state of resource authorization policy. If C(absent) will ensure the policy is removed. If C(present) will ensure the policy is configured and exists. If C(enabled) will ensure the policy is configured, exists and enabled. If C(disabled) will ensure the policy is configured, exists, but disabled.

fields.label: user groups
fields.description: List of user groups that are associated with this resource authorization policy (RAP). A user must belong to one of these groups to access the RD Gateway server. Required when a new RAP is created.

modules.label: win_rds_settings
modules.tooltip: Manage main settings of a Remote Desktop Gateway server

fields.label: certificate hash
fields.description: Certificate hash (thumbprint) for the Remote Desktop Gateway server. The certificate hash is the unique identifier for the certificate.

fields.label: enable only messaging capable clients
fields.description: If enabled, only clients that support logon messages and administrator messages can connect.

fields.label: max connections
fields.description: The maximum number of connections allowed. If set to C(0), no new connections are allowed. If set to C(-1), the number of connections is unlimited.

fields.label: ssl bridging
fields.description: Specifies whether to use SSL Bridging. C(none): no SSL bridging. C(https_http): HTTPS-HTTP bridging. C(https_https): HTTPS-HTTPS bridging.

modules.label: win_regmerge
modules.tooltip: Merges the contents of a registry file into the Windows registry

fields.label: compare to
fields.description: The parent key to use when comparing the contents of the registry to the contents of the file.  Needs to be in HKLM or HKCU part of registry. Use a PS-Drive style path for example HKLM:\SOFTWARE not HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE If not supplied, or the registry key is not found, no comparison will be made, and the module will report changed.

fields.label: content
fields.description: When used instead of O(path), merges the value specified into the Windows registry. It must not include the Byte Order Mark.

fields.label: path
fields.description: The full path including file name to the registry file on the remote machine to be merged

modules.label: win_robocopy
modules.tooltip: Synchronizes the contents of two directories using Robocopy

fields.label: dest
fields.description: Destination file/directory to sync (Will receive contents of src).

fields.label: flags
fields.description: Directly supply Robocopy flags. If set, C(purge) and C(recurse) will be ignored.

fields.label: purge
fields.description: Deletes any files/directories found in the destination that do not exist in the source. Toggles the C(/purge) flag to RoboCopy. If C(flags) is set, this will be ignored.

fields.label: recurse
fields.description: Includes all subdirectories (Toggles the C(/e) flag to RoboCopy). If C(flags) is set, this will be ignored.

fields.label: src
fields.description: Source file/directory to sync.

modules.label: win_say
modules.tooltip: Text to speech module for Windows to speak messages and optionally play sounds

fields.label: end sound path
fields.description: Full path to a C(.wav) file containing a sound to play after the text has been spoken. Useful on conference calls to alert other speakers that ansible has finished speaking.

fields.label: msg
fields.description: The text to be spoken. Use either C(msg) or C(msg_file). Optional so that you can use this module just to play sounds.

fields.label: msg file
fields.description: Full path to a windows format text file containing the text to be spoken. Use either C(msg) or C(msg_file). Optional so that you can use this module just to play sounds.

fields.label: speech speed
fields.description: How fast or slow to speak the text. Must be an integer value in the range -10 to 10. -10 is slowest, 10 is fastest.

fields.label: start sound path
fields.description: Full path to a C(.wav) file containing a sound to play before the text is spoken. Useful on conference calls to alert other speakers that ansible has something to say.

fields.label: voice
fields.description: Which voice to use. See notes for how to discover installed voices. If the requested voice is not available the default voice will be used. Example voice names from Windows 10 are C(Microsoft Zira Desktop) and C(Microsoft Hazel Desktop).

modules.label: win_scheduled_task
modules.tooltip: Manage scheduled tasks

fields.label: allow demand start
fields.description: Whether the task can be started by using either the Run command or the Context menu.

fields.label: allow hard terminate
fields.description: Whether the task can be terminated by using TerminateProcess.

fields.label: author
fields.description: The author of the task.

fields.label: compatibility
fields.description: The integer value with indicates which version of Task Scheduler a task is compatible with. C(0) means the task is compatible with the AT command. C(1) means the task is compatible with Task Scheduler 1.0(Windows Vista, Windows Server 2008 and older). C(2) means the task is compatible with Task Scheduler 2.0(Windows Vista, Windows Server 2008). C(3) means the task is compatible with Task Scheduler 2.0(Windows 7, Windows Server 2008 R2). C(4) means the task is compatible with Task Scheduler 2.0(Windows 10, Windows Server 2016, Windows Server 2019).

fields.label: date
fields.description: The date when the task was registered.

fields.label: delete expired task after
fields.description: The amount of time that the Task Scheduler will wait before deleting the task after it expires. A task expires after the end_boundary has been exceeded for all triggers associated with the task. This is in the ISO 8601 Duration format C(P[n]Y[n]M[n]DT[n]H[n]M[n]S).

fields.label: description
fields.description: The description of the task.

fields.label: disallow start if on batteries
fields.description: Whether the task will not be started if the computer is running on battery power.

fields.label: display name
fields.description: The name of the user/group that is displayed in the Task Scheduler UI.

fields.label: enabled
fields.description: Whether the task is enabled, the task can only run when C(yes).

fields.label: execution time limit
fields.description: The amount of time allowed to complete the task. When set to C(PT0S), the time limit is infinite. When omitted, the default time limit is 72 hours. This is in the ISO 8601 Duration format C(P[n]Y[n]M[n]DT[n]H[n]M[n]S).

fields.label: group
fields.description: The group that will run the task. C(group) and C(username) are exclusive to each other and cannot be set at the same time. C(logon_type) can either be not set or equal C(group).

fields.label: hidden
fields.description: Whether the task will be hidden in the UI.

fields.label: logon type
fields.description: The logon method that the task will run with. C(password) means the password will be stored and the task has access to network resources. C(s4u) means the existing token will be used to run the task and no password will be stored with the task. Means no network or encrypted files access. C(interactive_token) means the user must already be logged on interactively and will run in an existing interactive session. C(group) means that the task will run as a group. C(service_account) means that a service account like System, Local Service or Network Service will run the task.

fields.label: multiple instances
fields.description: An integer that indicates the behaviour when starting a task that is already running. C(0) will start a new instance in parallel with existing instances of that task. C(1) will wait until other instances of that task to finish running before starting itself. C(2) will not start a new instance if another is running. C(3) will stop other instances of the task and start the new one.

fields.label: name
fields.description: The name of the scheduled task without the path.

fields.label: path
fields.description: Task folder in which this task will be stored. Will create the folder when C(state=present) and the folder does not already exist. Will remove the folder when C(state=absent) and there are no tasks left in the folder.

fields.label: priority
fields.description: The priority level (0-10) of the task. When creating a new task the default is C(7). See U(https://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/windows/desktop/aa383512.aspx) for details on the priority levels.

fields.label: restart count
fields.description: The number of times that the Task Scheduler will attempt to restart the task.

fields.label: restart interval
fields.description: How long the Task Scheduler will attempt to restart the task. If this is set then C(restart_count) must also be set. The maximum allowed time is 31 days. The minimum allowed time is 1 minute. This is in the ISO 8601 Duration format C(P[n]Y[n]M[n]DT[n]H[n]M[n]S).

fields.label: run level
fields.description: The level of user rights used to run the task. If not specified the task will be created with limited rights.

fields.label: run only if idle
fields.description: Whether the task will run the task only if the computer is in an idle state.

fields.label: run only if network available
fields.description: Whether the task will run only when a network is available.

fields.label: source
fields.description: The source of the task.

fields.label: start when available
fields.description: Whether the task can start at any time after its scheduled time has passed.

fields.label: state
fields.description: When C(state=present) will ensure the task exists. When C(state=absent) will ensure the task does not exist.

fields.label: stop if going on batteries
fields.description: Whether the task will be stopped if the computer begins to run on battery power.

fields.label: update password
fields.description: Whether to update the password even when not other changes have occurred. When C(yes) will always result in a change when executing the module.

fields.label: version
fields.description: The version number of the task.

fields.label: wake to run
fields.description: Whether the task will wake the computer when it is time to run the task.

modules.label: win_scheduled_task_stat
modules.tooltip: Get information about Windows Scheduled Tasks

fields.label: name
fields.description: The name of the scheduled task to get information for. If C(name) is set and exists, will return information on the task itself.

fields.label: path
fields.description: The folder path where the task lives.

modules.label: win_scoop
modules.tooltip: Manage packages using Scoop

fields.label: architecture
fields.description: Force Scoop to install the package of a specific process architecture.

fields.label: global
fields.description: Install the app globally

fields.label: independent
fields.description: Don't install dependencies automatically

fields.label: name
fields.description: Name of the package(s) to be installed.

fields.label: no cache
fields.description: Don't use the download cache

fields.label: purge
fields.description: Remove all persistent data

fields.label: skip checksum
fields.description: Skip hash validation

fields.label: state
fields.description: State of the package on the system. When C(absent), will ensure the package is not installed. When C(present), will ensure the package is installed.

modules.label: win_scoop_bucket
modules.tooltip: Manage Scoop buckets

fields.label: name
fields.description: Name of the Scoop bucket.

fields.label: repo
fields.description: Git repository that contains the scoop bucket

fields.label: state
fields.description: State of the Scoop bucket. When C(absent), will ensure the package is not installed. When C(present), will ensure the package is installed.

modules.label: win_security_policy
modules.tooltip: Change local security policy settings

fields.label: key
fields.description: The ini key of the section or policy name to modify. The module will return an error if this key is invalid.

fields.label: section
fields.description: The ini section the key exists in. If the section does not exist then the module will return an error. Example sections to use are 'Account Policies', 'Local Policies', 'Event Log', 'Restricted Groups', 'System Services', 'Registry' and 'File System' If wanting to edit the C(Privilege Rights) section, use the M(ansible.windows.win_user_right) module instead.

fields.label: value
fields.description: The value for the ini key or policy name. If the key takes in a boolean value then 0 = False and 1 = True.

modules.label: win_shortcut
modules.tooltip: Manage shortcuts on Windows

fields.label: arguments
fields.description: Additional arguments for the executable defined in C(src).

fields.label: description
fields.description: Description for the shortcut. This is usually shown when hoovering the icon.

fields.label: dest
fields.description: Destination file for the shortcuting file. File name should have a C(.lnk) or C(.url) extension.

fields.label: directory
fields.description: Working directory for executable defined in C(src).

fields.label: hotkey
fields.description: Key combination for the shortcut. This is a combination of one or more modifiers and a key. Possible modifiers are Alt, Ctrl, Shift, Ext. Possible keys are [A-Z] and [0-9].

fields.label: icon
fields.description: Icon used for the shortcut. File name should have a C(.ico) extension. The file name is followed by a comma and the number in the library file (.dll) or use 0 for an image file.

fields.label: run as admin
fields.description: When C(src) is an executable, this can control whether the shortcut will be opened as an administrator or not.

fields.label: src
fields.description: Executable or URL the shortcut points to. The executable needs to be in your PATH, or has to be an absolute path to the executable.

fields.label: state
fields.description: When C(absent), removes the shortcut if it exists. When C(present), creates or updates the shortcut.

fields.label: windowstyle
fields.description: Influences how the application is displayed when it is launched.

modules.label: win_snmp
modules.tooltip: Configures the Windows SNMP service

fields.label: action
fields.description: C(add) will add new SNMP community strings and/or SNMP managers C(set) will replace SNMP community strings and/or SNMP managers. An empty list for either C(community_strings) or C(permitted_managers) will result in the respective lists being removed entirely. C(remove) will remove SNMP community strings and/or SNMP managers

fields.label: community strings
fields.description: The list of read-only SNMP community strings.

fields.label: permitted managers
fields.description: The list of permitted SNMP managers.

modules.label: win_toast
modules.tooltip: Sends Toast windows notification to logged in users on Windows 10 or later hosts

fields.label: expire
fields.description: How long in seconds before the notification expires.

fields.label: group
fields.description: Which notification group to add the notification to.

fields.label: msg
fields.description: The message to appear inside the notification. May include \n to format the message to appear within the Action Center.

fields.label: popup
fields.description: If C(no), the notification will not pop up and will only appear in the Action Center.

fields.label: tag
fields.description: The tag to add to the notification.

fields.label: title
fields.description: The notification title, which appears in the pop up..

modules.label: win_unzip
modules.tooltip: Unzips compressed files and archives on the Windows node

fields.label: creates
fields.description: If this file or directory exists the specified src will not be extracted.

fields.label: delete archive
fields.description: Remove the zip file, after unzipping.

fields.label: dest
fields.description: Destination of zip file (provide absolute path of directory). If it does not exist, the directory will be created.

fields.label: recurse
fields.description: Recursively expand zipped files within the src file. Setting to a value of C(yes) requires the PSCX module to be installed.

fields.label: src
fields.description: File to be unzipped (provide absolute path).

modules.label: win_wait_for_process
modules.tooltip: Waits for a process to exist or not exist before continuing.

fields.label: owner
fields.description: The owner of the process. Requires PowerShell version 4.0 or newer.

fields.label: pid
fields.description: The PID of the process.

fields.label: post wait delay
fields.description: Seconds to wait after checking for processes.

fields.label: pre wait delay
fields.description: Seconds to wait before checking processes.

fields.label: process min count
fields.description: Minimum number of process matching the supplied pattern to satisfy C(present) condition. Only applies to C(present).

fields.label: process name exact
fields.description: The name of the process(es) for which to wait.  The name of the process(es) should not include the file extension suffix.

fields.label: process name pattern
fields.description: RegEx pattern matching desired process(es).

fields.label: sleep
fields.description: Number of seconds to sleep between checks. Only applies when waiting for a process to start.  Waiting for a process to start does not have a native non-polling mechanism. Waiting for a stop uses native PowerShell and does not require polling.

fields.label: state
fields.description: When checking for a running process C(present) will block execution until the process exists, or until the timeout has been reached. C(absent) will block execution until the process no longer exists, or until the timeout has been reached. When waiting for C(present), the module will return changed only if the process was not present on the initial check but became present on subsequent checks. If, while waiting for C(absent), new processes matching the supplied pattern are started, these new processes will not be included in the action.

modules.label: win_wakeonlan
modules.tooltip: Send a magic Wake-on-LAN (WoL) broadcast packet

fields.label: broadcast
fields.description: Network broadcast address to use for broadcasting magic Wake-on-LAN packet.

fields.label: mac
fields.description: MAC address to send Wake-on-LAN broadcast packet for.

modules.label: win_webpicmd
modules.tooltip: Installs packages using Web Platform Installer command-line

fields.label: name
fields.description: Name of the package to be installed.

modules.label: win_xml
modules.tooltip: Manages XML file content on Windows hosts

fields.label: attribute
fields.description: The attribute name if the type is 'attribute'. Required if C(type=attribute).

fields.label: backup
fields.description: Determine whether a backup should be created. When set to C(yes), create a backup file including the timestamp information so you can get the original file back if you somehow clobbered it incorrectly.

fields.label: content
fields.description: Returns the content of the nodes matched by I(xpath).

fields.label: count
fields.description: When set to C(yes), return the number of nodes matched by I(xpath).

fields.label: fragment
fields.description: The string representation of the XML fragment expected at xpath.  Since ansible 2.9 not required when I(state=absent) or when I(count=yes). Not required when I(content) is set.

fields.label: path
fields.description: Path to the file to operate on.

fields.label: preserve whitespace
fields.description: Whether to preserve whitespace in the XML.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Set or remove the nodes (or attributes) matched by I(xpath).

fields.label: type
fields.description: The type of XML node you are working with.

fields.label: xpath
fields.description: Xpath to select the node or nodes to operate on.

modules.label: win_zip
modules.tooltip: Compress file or directory as zip archive on the Windows node

fields.label: dest
fields.description: Destination path of zip file (provide absolute path of zip file on the target node).

fields.label: src
fields.description: File or directory path to be zipped (provide absolute path on the target node). When a directory path the directory is zipped as the root entry in the archive. Specify C(\*) to the end of I(src) to zip the contents of the directory and not the directory itself.
